webUrl: MCLN EX100 - Inbound ASN Not Verified Before Sync V.01 .docx
title: MCLN EX100 - Inbound ASN Not Verified Before Sync V.01
fullText:
313690 344805 0 0 EX100 - I nbound ASN N ot V erified B efore S ync Contents TOC \o "1-2" \z \u \h 1 Enhancement Revision History PAGEREF _Toc177712562 \h 3 2 EX100 - Inbound ASN Not Verified Before Sync PAGEREF _Toc177712563 \h 4 2.1 Summary Information PAGEREF _Toc177712564 \h 4 2.2 Overview PAGEREF _Toc177712565 \h 4 2.3 Assumptions PAGEREF _Toc177712566 \h 4 2.4 Platform Information PAGEREF _Toc177712567 \h 4 3 Process Flow Diagram PAGEREF _Toc177712568 \h 4 4 UI PAGEREF _Toc177712569 \h 5 5 Process Change/Addition PAGEREF _Toc177712570 \h 5 6 Reporting, Label, or Document PAGEREF _Toc177712571 \h 7 7 Other Solutions Included in Extension PAGEREF _Toc177712572 \h 7 8 Performance Expectations PAGEREF _Toc177712573 \h 7 9 Required Data Setup PAGEREF _Toc177712574 \h 7 9.1 Menu Parameters PAGEREF _Toc177712575 \h 7 9.2 Warehouse Master PAGEREF _Toc177712576 \h 7 9.3 System Codes PAGEREF _Toc177712577 \h 7 9.4 Database PAGEREF _Toc177712578 \h 8 9.5 Custom Configurations PAGEREF _Toc177712579 \h 8 10 Questions and Answers PAGEREF _Toc177712580 \h 8 Enhancement Revision History Changed By Date Version Notes: Co dy Ramangkoun 9 / 12 /202 4 0. 01 Original Document EX100 - I nbound ASN N ot V erified B efore S ync MCLN has items that are going out of sync between MCLN host/MANH Host systems due to the Inbound ASN not being verified prior to the sync that happens at 3:30pm EST (can differ depending on warehouse). If MCLN verifies the received ASN after the allotted scheduled sync time, then MCLN faces inventory sync issues between MLCN Host/MANH Host. In order to maintain inventory syncs between the two host systems, MANH is customized to add custom activity tracking record REF_FIELD_4 ( PROD_TRKG_TRAN .REF_FIELD_4) and REF_CODE_ID_4 ( PROD_TRKG_TRAN .REF_CODE_ID_4) to transaction type 300 with transaction codes “Replenished Active” and “Replenished Case Pick” in order to grab the necessary ASN Number and allow the ASN to get stamped onto the activity tracking detail record . Th e stamping of both custom fields are both DB and UI visib le. Summary Information Customer Name: McLane Customer Code: MCLN Subproject Codes: TBD Change Request Number: Overview Functional Area Enhanced Additional Information User Interface (UI) Y MHE N RF N Reporting N Other N DB Column Updates Assumptions iLPNs are received with an ASN_ID iLPNS have 1 unique ASN_ID EX100 only covers INT1/9 replenishment tasks, any additional replenishment INT group is considered out of scope Interface Dependencies Interface Field Requirement Platform Information Solution: Solution Platform: Solution Version: Warehouse Management Open Systems Oracle/ Tomcat /RHEL 201 9 Process Flow Diagram Full Pallet Replenishment User launches RF ASN Receiving Transactions to begin Receiving Process WMOS receives a n item containing ASN_ID field within a n ASN /shipment .   User completes the receiving process as needed User runs standard fill wave to generate replenishment tasks with the iLPN received by the received ASN User signs into RF Menu and into a replenishment task group to begin the replenishment process User enters into a replenishment task and prompted for iLPN scan. User scans iLPN User is prompted for ALOC/ CLOC , user scans ALOC/ CLOC . Activity tracking trans type 300 , trans code record “replenish case pick” or “replenish active” generate s via base for task. IF EX100 is enabled THEN, WMOS updates custom field PROD_TRKG_TRAN.REF_ CODE_ID _4 with “ASN Number ” and PROD_TRKG_TRAN.REF _FIELD_4 with the value populated in < ASN . TC_ASN_ID  > for that iLPN once activity tracking record trans type 300 , trans code “replenish case pick” or “replenish active” is created. ELSE IF, continue as per base processing Partial Pallet Replenishment User launches RF ASN Receiving Transactions to begin Receiving Process WMOS receives a n item containing ASN_ID field within a n ASN/shipment .   User completes the receiving process as needed User runs standard fill wave to generate replenishment tasks with the iLPN received by the received ASN User signs into RF Menu and into a replenishment task group to begin the replenishment process User enters into a replenishment task and prompted for iLPN scan. User scans iLPN User is prompted for quantity, user enters quantity User is prompted to scan blind iLPN , user scans blind iLPN IF EX100 is enabled THEN WMOS updates the LPN.TC_ASN_ID field on the blind iLPN with the TC_ASN_ID value from the original iLPN User scans drop location User scans iLPN User is prompted for ALOC/CLOC, user scans ALOC/CLOC. User is prompted for quantity, user enters quantity User Activity tracking trans type 300, trans code record “replenish case pick” or “replenish active” generates via base for task. IF EX100 is enabled THEN, WMOS updates custom field PROD_TRKG_TRAN.REF_ CODE_ID _4 with “ASN Number” and PROD_TRKG_TRAN.REF _FIELD_4 with the value populated in < ASN. TC_ASN_ID  > for that iLPN once activity tracking record trans type 300, trans code “replenish case pick” or “replenish active” is created. ELSE IF, continue as per base processing UI No UI changes in scope for this extension. Enablement The following section describes the custom configuration required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing will occur. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX 100 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX 100 is turned off, and no custom EX 100 processing will occur. Process Change/Addition The import processes for ASNs ar e modified to include the below base field, which is used as a reference field during import to enter ASN_ID value into WMOS. This is located within the < ASN > section.     Field Name   Data Type   Description   TC_ ASN_ID   Varchar( 20)  Contains unique identifier ASN_ID value   If that field is null or not present for the iLPN allocated during the wave, no further updates are made . If the field contains a value, WMOS will use the value held there to fill PROD_TRKG_TRAN .REF_FIELD_4 for transaction types 300, transaction code “Replenished Active” and “Replenished Case Pick” . WM will also customize PROD_TRKG_TRAN .REF_CODE_ID_4 to display the value title (ASN Number ) of what is contained in REF_FIELD_4 to the user.   Table Name   Column Name   Data Type   Description   Nullable?   PROD_TRKG_TRAN REF_CODE_ID_4 Varchar( 20) Represents the value title contained in REF_FIELD _4 . Value title = “ASN Number ” Y PROD_TRKG_TRAN REF_FIELD _4 V archar( 20) Contains unique identifier ASN_ID value Y  Updates No custom updates are made as part of this extension. Validations Activity Tracking WMOS will use the value held in ASN.TC_ASN_ID for the iLPN to fill PROD_TRKG_TRAN . REF_FIELD_4 for transaction types 300, transaction code “Replenished Active” and “Replenished Case Pick” once the activity tracking record is generated . Field Name Definition Custom TRAN_TYPE 300 – Pulling/Replenishment No TRAN_CODE 00 5 – Replenished Active , 007 – Replenish Case Pick No TRAN_NBR The transaction Number nam e No SEQ_NBR The current Sequence Number No WHSE The Current Facility No CNTR_NBR The ILPN ID No WAVE_NBR The wave number of the current TASK ID No PKT_CTRL_NBR The Picker Controller Number No PKT_SEQ_NBR The Picking Sequence Number No WORK_TYPE NULL No NBR_OF_CASES NULL No NBR_UNITS The number of Units No NBR_OF_PIKS NULL No NBR_SCAN NULL No FROM_LOCN The Location from where the activity was begun Yes TO_LOCN The Location from where the activity was begun Yes WKSTN_ID NULL No RSN_CODE NULL Yes REF_CODE_ID_1 NULL No REF_FIELD_1 NULL No REF_CODE_ID_2 NULL No REF_FIELD_2 NULL No REF_CODE_ID_3 NULL No REF_FIELD_3 NULL No REF_CODE_ID_4 “ASN Number ” Yes REF_FIELD_4 ASN.TC_ASN_ID Yes REF_CODE_ID_5 NULL No REF_FIELD_5 NULL No OLD_STAT_CODE NULL No NEW_STAT_CODE NULL No SAM NULL No BEGIN_DATE System Date/Time when user started transaction No END_DATE System Date/Time when user ended transaction No STAT_CODE The Stat Code of the task No MODULE_NAME The current Module Name of task No MENU_OPTN_NAME The current Menu Option Name of task No CREATE_DATE_TIME Current System Date/Time No MOD_DATE_TIME Current System Date/Time No USER_ID The Current User ID No PLT_ID The current Pallet ID No TASK_ID The current Task ID No CD_MASTER_ID The current CD Master ID No PROD_TRKG_TRAN_ID The current product tracking tran ID No WM_VERSION_ID The current WM Version ID No ITEM_ID The item ID from the message. No TASK_HDR_ID The current Task ID No TO_LOCN_HDR_ID The current location ID of the Task. No TC_ORDER_ID The order associated to the task No LINE_ITEM_ID The line item ID associated to the task No FROM_LPN NULL Yes PIX No changes to base behavior for PIX with the wave allocation as part of this extension. LM Hooks No changes to base behavior for labor management with the wave allocation as part of this extension. Reporting, Label, or Document Not report, label, or document changes in scope for this extension. Other Solutions Included in Extension Not in scope for this extension. Performance Expectations The p urpose of this section is to identify performance requirements up front . Process Step Peak Transaction Rate Max Response 95% Response Recovery Queue Recovery Time Operations Driver & notes Required Data Setup All necessary data setup for the extension to function accurately are detailed below.
